title: 11 Best Herbal Teas For Lice
intro: Herbal teas for lice are natural remedies made from various herbs and plants that have been used to treat and eliminate lice infestations. These teas can treat lice because they contain compounds that suffocate the lice, prevent their eggs from hatching, or repel them altogether. Examples of herbal teas that can be used to treat lice include tea tree oil, which has antiparasitic properties, peppermint tea, which can help soothe and calm the scalp, lavender tea, which has antiseptic properties that can help eliminate bacteria and fungi associated with lice, chamomile tea, which can help reduce itchiness and inflammation, and neem tea, which has natural insecticidal properties that can kill lice and their eggs. Additionally, other herbal teas such as eucalyptus tea, rosemary tea, and lemongrass tea have also been used to repel and eliminate lice due to their strong and pungent scents.

preparations.herb_desc: Melaleuca alternifolia teas, also known as tea tree oil infusions, have been traditionally used to treat lice and other scalp-related issues due to their antiparasitic and antiseptic properties. The bioactive constituents of this herbal preparation, including cineole and terpinen-4-ol, help to repel lice and their eggs by creating an environment that is inhospitable to their survival. This herbal preparation helps to treat lice by killing the lice and their eggs, reducing the risk of re-infestation and promoting a healthy scalp environment. The benefits of using Melaleuca alternifolia teas to treat lice include reduced itching, inflammation, and discomfort, making it a popular natural remedy for this common scalp issue.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Melaleuca alternifolia leaves and flowers.
Boil 2 cups of water in a pot.
Add 1 tablespoon of dried Melaleuca alternifolia leaves to the boiling water.
Reduce heat and let it steep for 5-10 minutes, then strain the liquid.
Mix 1 part of the tea with 1 part of water to make a final solution. Use this solution to rinse the hair and scalp after shampooing.
preparations.preparation_study: According to "Fitoterapia", Melaleuca alternifolia teas for lice are effective, with tea tree oil being the most effective essential oil against both lice and mites.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Melaleuca alternifolia teas can be used to treat lice ailments, but its use may cause side effects such as skin irritation, allergic reactions, and gastrointestinal issues in some individuals. When using Melaleuca alternifolia teas to treat lice, it is essential to take precautions, including avoiding eye contact, using a diluted solution, and applying a small patch test before full application to prevent any adverse reactions.
preparation_usage: Soak hair in 1 cup of warm water with 2 tablespoons of Melaleuca alternifolia tea.
Leave the mixture on for 1-2 hours or overnight.
Use a fine-tooth comb to remove dead lice and nits.
Repeat the treatment every 2-3 days for 2 weeks.
Shampoo hair with regular shampoo after each treatment.

preparations.herb_desc: Rosmarinus officinalis teas, a herbal preparation derived from the rosemary plant, have been traditionally used to treat lice infestations due to their potent insecticidal and antiparasitic properties. The bioactive constituents of this herbal preparation, including camphor, bornyl acetate, and rosmarinic acid, contribute to its effectiveness in treating lice by repelling and killing the lice and their eggs. These constituents help to disrupt the lice's life cycle and prevent re-infestation, ultimately leading to a reduction in lice populations and alleviation of associated discomfort and itching. The benefits of using Rosmarinus officinalis teas to treat lice include its natural, non-toxic, and cost-effective approach, making it an attractive alternative to traditional chemical-based treatments.
preparation_procedure: Gather ingredients: 1 cup of fresh or dried Rosmarinus officinalis leaves, 1 cup of boiling water, and a strainer.
Measure 1 tablespoon of Rosmarinus officinalis leaves into a cup.
Pour boiling water over the leaves and let it steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea into a separate cup to remove the leaves.
Let the tea cool down before applying it to the hair as a treatment for lice. Use as directed and repeat as needed.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Rosmarinus officinalis teas can be used to treat lice, but it may cause side effects such as allergic reactions, skin irritation, and increased heart rate due to the presence of volatile oils. Precautions should be taken, including avoiding the use of Rosmarinus officinalis teas on broken skin, on children under the age of 6, and during pregnancy or breastfeeding, as the essential oils may be absorbed through the skin and cause harm to the developing fetus or baby.
preparation_usage: Use 2 cups of boiling water to make 1 cup of Rosmarinus officinalis tea
Drink 1/4 cup of tea 3 times a day for 7 days
Wash hair with mild shampoo 2 times a day for 7 days
Use a fine-tooth comb to remove lice and nits daily
Repeat process for 2 weeks for effective lice removal

preparation_amazon.title: Palm Beach Medicinal Herbs - Rosemary Tea - Pure Herbal Tea Series, 30ct

preparations.herb_desc: Eucalyptus globulus teas have been traditionally used to treat lice infestations due to their anti-parasitic and insecticidal properties. The bioactive constituents present in this herbal preparation, including eucalyptol, limonene, and pinene, help to repel and kill lice by disrupting their nervous system and preventing egg-laying. These constituents also aid in reducing itching and inflammation caused by lice bites, providing relief to affected individuals. The benefits of using Eucalyptus globulus teas to treat lice include its natural and non-toxic nature, ease of preparation, and ability to provide quick relief from lice infestations.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Eucalyptus globulus leaves.
Add 1 tablespoon of dried Eucalyptus globulus leaves to 1 cup of boiling water.
Steep the leaves for 5-7 minutes, then strain the mixture.
Combine the strained mixture with 1 cup of water and bring to a boil.
Let the tea cool and use it as a final rinse after shampooing to help repel lice.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Eucalyptus globulus teas can be used to treat lice ailments, but using them may cause side effects such as allergic reactions, skin irritation, and digestive issues in some individuals. When using Eucalyptus globulus teas to treat lice, it is essential to take precautions, such as using it in moderation, avoiding contact with sensitive areas, and performing a patch test before applying it to the affected area.
preparation_usage: Check lice infestation and consult doctor if necessary.
Shower with regular shampoo and rinse thoroughly.
Mix 2 tablespoons of Eucalyptus globulus oil with 1 cup of coconut oil.
Apply mixture to hair and scalp, leave for 30 minutes to 1 hour.
Repeat treatment 2-3 times a week for 2-3 weeks, with daily shampooing.

preparation_amazon.title: Jovvily Eucalyptus Leaves - 1lb - Dried - Cut & Sifted - Herbal Tea

preparations.herb_desc: Artemisia absinthium teas have been traditionally used to treat lice infestations due to their potent antiparasitic and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to soothe the scalp and eliminate the lice. The herbal preparation's bioactive constituents, including thujone, camphor, and borneol, work synergistically to repel and kill lice, making it an effective treatment for this ailment. By using Artemisia absinthium teas, individuals can benefit from a natural and non-toxic solution that not only eliminates the lice but also helps to prevent their recurrence and reduce the risk of infestation. The antimicrobial and antiseptic properties of this herbal preparation also help to prevent secondary infections and promote a healthy scalp environment.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh or dried Artemisia absinthium leaves.
Measure 2 tablespoons of Artemisia absinthium leaves and place them in a heat-resistant cup.
Pour 1 cup of boiling water over the leaves and let it steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into another cup, discarding the solids.
Use the tea as a final rinse after shampooing your hair to help repel lice.
preparations.preparation_study: According to the Iranian Red Crescent medical journal, Artemisia absinthium teas for lice have been identified as having insecticidal activity, based on traditional Iranian medicine claims that have been supported by modern investigations.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Artemisia absinthium teas can be used as a treatment for lice, but it may cause side effects such as allergic reactions, skin irritation, and gastrointestinal issues due to its thujone content. Precautions should be taken when using this herbal preparation, including avoiding excessive consumption, as it may lead to headaches, dizziness, and other neurological symptoms, and ensuring that the tea is brewed at a safe temperature to prevent burns.
preparation_usage: Mix 2 tablespoons of dried Artemisia absinthium with 1 cup of boiling water.
Soak the hair with the tea for 30 minutes to 1 hour.
Rinse the hair thoroughly with warm water and shampoo.
Repeat the process 2-3 times a week for 2-3 weeks.
Use a fine-tooth comb to remove lice and eggs after each treatment.

preparations.herb_desc: Cymbopogon citratus teas, also known as lemongrass tea, have been traditionally used to treat lice infestations due to their potent insecticidal and anti-parasitic properties. The tea's active compounds, including citral and geraniol, help to repel and kill lice, as well as their eggs, thereby treating the ailment effectively. The bioactive constituents present in lemongrass tea, such as limonene and beta-pinene, contribute to its lice-repelling properties, making it a popular natural remedy for this condition. The benefits of using lemongrass tea to treat lice infestations include its non-toxic and environmentally friendly nature, making it a safer alternative to chemical-based treatments.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Cymbopogon citratus leaves or 1 tablespoon of dried leaves.
Combine the leaves with 1 cup of boiling water in a saucepan.
Reduce heat and let it simmer for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the mixture into a cup and discard the leaves.
Drink the tea twice a day, 1/4 cup each time, for 7-10 days to help eliminate lice.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Cymbopogon citratus teas can be used as a potential treatment for lice, but it may cause side effects such as skin irritation, allergic reactions, and digestive issues in some individuals. When using Cymbopogon citratus teas to treat lice, precautions should be taken to avoid applying excessive amounts to the skin, as this can cause increased irritation and potentially lead to infections, especially in individuals with open wounds or compromised skin.
preparation_usage: Drink 1 cup of Cymbopogon citratus tea 3 times a day for 7 days.
Add 2-3 drops of tea essential oil to shampoo or hair conditioner.
Apply the tea-infused shampoo to hair, leave on for 30 minutes.
Repeat shampooing and rinsing with warm water 2-3 times a week.
Comb hair with fine-tooth comb to remove lice and nits daily.

preparations.herb_desc: Lavandula angustifolia teas have been used for centuries to treat various health issues, including lice infestation. The antiparasitic properties of this herbal preparation help to treat lice by disrupting the life cycle of the parasites and reducing their reproduction rate. The bioactive constituents of Lavandula angustifolia, including linalool and linalyl acetate, exhibit potent insecticidal and antifungal activities that aid in treating the lice ailment. The benefits of using Lavandula angustifolia teas to treat lice include a natural and gentle approach, reduced risk of resistance, and minimal side effects, making it a popular alternative to chemical-based treatments.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of dried Lavandula angustifolia flowers.
Add 1 quart of boiling water to a saucepan.
Steep the dried flowers in the boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the liquid and discard the flowers.
Use the cooled tea as a final rinse after shampooing your hair to help repel lice.
preparations.preparation_study: According to "Fitoterapia", Lavandula angustifolia teas for lice show acaricidal and pediculicidal activity, being the second most effective essential oil against both lice and house dust mites after tea tree oil.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Lavandula angustifolia teas can be used as a natural remedy to treat lice, but it may cause skin irritation, allergic reactions, and stomach upset in some individuals. When using Lavandula angustifolia teas to treat lice, it is essential to take precautions such as performing a patch test before application, avoiding contact with sensitive areas, and using a gentle shampoo to minimize potential side effects.
preparation_usage: Mix 2 tablespoons of dried Lavandula angustifolia with 1 cup of boiling water.
Let the tea steep for 5-7 minutes, then strain it.
Shampoo your hair with a medicated lice shampoo, then rinse.
Apply the Lavandula angustifolia tea as a final rinse, 2 times a week.
Repeat the treatment for 2 consecutive weeks to ensure lice removal.

preparation_amazon.title: Tiesta Tea - Lavender Chamomile Herbal Tea | Loose Leaf | Calming Blend with Chamomile and Lavender | Caffeine-Free Herbal | Great for Hot or Iced Brews | Resealable Bulk Pouch, 200 Cups | 8 Ounce

preparations.herb_desc: Thymus serpyllum teas, also known as wild thyme, have been used traditionally to treat lice infestations due to their antiparasitic and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of wild thyme, such as thymol, carvacrol, and ursolic acid, help to repel and kill lice, as well as soothe the scalp and alleviate itching. This herbal preparation works by creating an environment that is unfavorable for the lice to survive, ultimately leading to their elimination, and also helps to reduce the risk of re-infestation. The benefits of using Thymus serpyllum teas to treat lice include its non-toxic and environmentally friendly nature, making it an attractive alternative to chemical-based treatments.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Thymus serpyllum leaves. You can grow them in your garden or buy from a store.
Wash the leaves with water to remove any dirt. Pat them dry with a cloth.
Steep 1 tablespoon of the dried leaves in 1 cup of boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the mixture and let it cool. You can add a sweetener like honey if you want.
Use the tea to wash your hair and scalp to help remove lice. Repeat the process daily for best results.
preparations.preparation_study: According to the study, Thymus serpyllum teas (also known as tea tree oil) were found to be the most effective against lice.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Thymus serpyllum teas can be used to treat lice, but it may cause side effects such as stomach upset, allergic reactions, or interactions with other medications. When using Thymus serpyllum teas to treat lice, it is essential to take precautions, such as avoiding consumption if pregnant or breastfeeding, and carefully monitoring your body's reaction to the herbal preparation.
preparation_usage: Dampen your hair with water for effective tea absorption.
Use 1-2 tablespoons of Thymus serpyllum tea for a cup of warm water.
Soak your hair in the tea mixture for 30-45 minutes.
Repeat the tea treatment 2-3 times a week for 2-3 weeks.
Comb your hair with a fine-tooth comb to remove lice and eggs.

preparations.herb_desc: Achillea millefolium teas have been traditionally used to treat lice infestations due to their antiparasitic and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to soothe the scalp and reduce itching. The bioactive constituents, including sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, contribute to the herbal preparation's ability to kill lice and their eggs, ultimately leading to the eradication of the infestation. The antiseptic properties of Achillea millefolium teas also help to prevent secondary infections and promote a healthy scalp environment. Regular use of Achillea millefolium teas as a natural remedy may offer benefits such as reduced reliance on chemical treatments, fewer side effects, and a more gentle approach to treating lice infestations.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Achillea millefolium leaves and flowers.
Dry the leaves and flowers completely by air-drying or using a food dehydrator.
Use 2 tablespoons of dried Achillea millefolium to make a tea.
Steep the dried herbs in 1 cup of boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea and use it as a natural treatment for lice, repeating the process as needed.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Achillea millefolium teas can be used to treat lice infestations, but possible side effects may include allergic reactions, skin irritation, and potential interactions with other medications. Precautions include avoiding use on broken skin, pregnant or breastfeeding women, and individuals with sensitive skin, as well as monitoring for signs of allergic reactions or increased heart rate.
preparation_usage: Mix 1 tablespoon of dried Achillea millefolium with 1 cup of boiling water.
Steep for 5-7 minutes, then strain the liquid.
Drink 2 cups of the tea per day, 2 times a day.
Use the tea for 2-3 weeks to help treat lice infestation.
Repeat the treatment after 1 week for effective results.

preparations.herb_desc: Ocimum basilicum teas, derived from the sweet basil plant, have been traditionally used to treat lice infestations due to their antiparasitic and anti-inflammatory properties. The herbal preparation helps to treat lice by killing the lice and their eggs, as well as relieving the itching and discomfort associated with the infestation. The bioactive constituents of Ocimum basilicum, including eugenol, linalool, and ursolic acid, have been shown to possess insecticidal and ovicidal properties, contributing to its effectiveness in treating lice. The benefits of using Ocimum basilicum teas to treat lice include a natural and non-toxic approach, reduced risk of developing resistance, and a soothing effect on the scalp, making it a popular choice for those seeking a herbal remedy.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Ocimum basilicum leaves and 1 cup of boiling water.
Steep the Ocimum basilicum leaves in the boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the mixture and add 2 tablespoons of honey to taste.
Add 2 tablespoons of apple cider vinegar to the mixture for extra benefits.
Drink the tea 2-3 times a day for 1 week to help eliminate lice.
preparations.preparation_study: According to "Environmental science and pollution research international", Ocimum basilicum teas for lice may be effective in killing lice due to the presence of compounds such as methyl chavicol, linalool, and epi-α-cadinol in the essential oil, which have been shown to have ovicidal and contact toxic effects.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Ocimum basilicum teas can be used as a natural remedy to treat lice, but it may cause side effects such as skin irritation, allergic reactions, and increased sensitivity to sunlight. When using Ocimum basilicum teas to treat lice, precautions should be taken to avoid overconsumption, which can lead to dizziness, nausea, and stomach upset, and to perform patch tests before applying the tea to the scalp to minimize the risk of an adverse reaction.
preparation_usage: Shampoo hair with regular shampoo before using basil tea treatment.
Mix 2 tablespoons of Ocimum basilicum tea with 1 cup of water.
Apply the basil tea mixture to the hair and scalp, 2 times a week.
Leave the solution on for 30 minutes, then rinse with warm water.
Repeat the treatment for 2-3 weeks to ensure complete lice removal.

preparations.herb_desc: Zingiber officinale teas, derived from the rhizome of the ginger plant, have been traditionally used to treat lice infestations due to their antiparasitic properties. The bioactive constituents, including gingerols and shogaols, help to repel and kill lice by disrupting their nervous system and preventing them from feeding on human blood. By inhibiting the lice's ability to multiply and survive, Zingiber officinale teas provide a natural and non-invasive treatment option for individuals suffering from lice infestations. The benefits of using Zingiber officinale teas to treat lice include reduced risk of chemical resistance, fewer side effects, and a more environmentally friendly approach to parasite control.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh ginger root and 2 cups of water.
Wash the ginger root and slice it thinly.
Boil the water in a pot and add the sliced ginger.
Reduce heat and let it simmer for 5 minutes, then let it cool.
Strain the liquid and drink 1 cup, 2-3 times a day, for 7-10 days.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Zingiber officinale teas can be used to treat lice infestation, but possible side effects include stomach upset, nausea, and diarrhea due to its high ginger content. To avoid these side effects, it's essential to start with a small dose, gradually increase as needed, and choose high-quality teas from reputable sources to minimize contamination risks.
preparation_usage: Steep 1 tablespoon of Zingiber officinale tea in 1 cup of boiling water for 5 minutes.
Let the tea cool down to a comfortable temperature for hair treatment.
Apply the cooled tea to the hair, focusing on the scalp and lice-infested areas.
Leave the tea on for 30 minutes to an hour before rinsing with warm water.
Repeat the process 2-3 times a week for 2-3 weeks to effectively eliminate lice.

preparations.herb_desc: Hyptis suaveolens teas have been traditionally used to treat lice infestations due to their insecticidal and antiparasitic properties, which help to repel and eliminate lice from the scalp. The bioactive constituents present in Hyptis suaveolens teas, including thymol, carvacrol, and eugenol, exhibit potent insecticidal and antiparasitic activities, making it an effective remedy for lice treatment. The antiparasitic properties of Hyptis suaveolens teas help to kill lice eggs and prevent their hatching, thereby providing long-term relief from the infestation. The benefits of using Hyptis suaveolens teas for lice treatment include their natural origin, non-toxicity, and cost-effectiveness, making it a popular alternative to conventional lice treatments.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of dried Hyptis suaveolens leaves.
Boil 2 cups of water in a pot.
Add 1 tablespoon of dried leaves to the boiling water.
Reduce heat and let it simmer for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea and let it cool before using it to treat lice.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Hyptis suaveolens teas can be used as a natural remedy to treat lice ailments, but it may cause side effects such as skin irritation, allergic reactions, and digestive issues in some individuals. When using Hyptis suaveolens teas to treat lice, take precautions by diluting the tea with water, avoiding contact with the eyes and sensitive areas, and performing a patch test before applying it to the affected areas.
preparation_usage: Wash hair with shampoo to remove dirt and lice
Make a tea with 1 cup of Hyptis suaveolens leaves and 1 cup of water
Soak hair in tea for 30 minutes to 1 hour
Repeat every 2 days for 2 weeks, then once a week
Use a fine-tooth comb to remove lice and eggs after each treatment

preparations.herb_desc: Saponaria officinalis teas have been traditionally used to treat lice infestations due to their antiparasitic and anti-inflammatory properties. The herbal preparation is believed to help treat lice by disrupting the life cycle of the lice and relieving the associated itching and discomfort. The bioactive constituents of Saponaria officinalis teas, including saponins and flavonoids, are thought to contribute to its antiparasitic activity, helping to kill the lice and their eggs. The benefits of using Saponaria officinalis teas to treat lice include a natural and non-toxic approach to managing the infestation, reducing the risk of chemical resistance and side effects associated with conventional treatments.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of dried Saponaria officinalis flowers and 1 cup of boiling water.
Steep the Saponaria officinalis flowers in the boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a clean cup.
Add 1 tablespoon of honey to the tea to help soothe the scalp.
Use the tea as a final rinse after shampooing your hair to help repel lice.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Saponaria officinalis teas can cause skin irritation, allergic reactions, or gastrointestinal upset in some individuals when used to treat lice infestations. Precautions should be taken to avoid applying Saponaria officinalis teas directly to the scalp, as excessive use or prolonged exposure may lead to irritation or other adverse effects.

preparations.herb_desc: Cinchona officinalis teas have been used for centuries to treat the lice ailment due to their antiparasitic and insecticidal properties. The bioactive constituents, including quinine and alkaloids, help to repel and kill lice, as well as their eggs, by disrupting their nervous system and preventing them from reproducing. This herbal preparation is also beneficial as it is a natural and non-toxic alternative to chemical-based treatments, reducing the risk of side effects and promoting a healthier scalp. The antiseptic properties of Cinchona officinalis teas also help to soothe and calm the scalp, reducing irritation and inflammation caused by the lice infestation.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of water, 2 tablespoons of dried Cinchona officinalis leaves, and a tea infuser or strainer.
Boil the water in a pot and remove from heat.
Add the dried Cinchona officinalis leaves to the tea infuser or strainer and place in the hot water.
Let the tea steep for 5-10 minutes, then strain and discard the leaves.
Let the tea cool and use it as a final rinse after shampooing to help repel lice.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Cinchona officinalis teas can be effective in treating lice, but they may cause side effects such as digestive issues, allergic reactions, or interactions with other medications due to its quinine content. Precautions when using Cinchona officinalis teas include starting with a small dose, monitoring for any adverse reactions, and avoiding use in pregnant or breastfeeding women, as well as individuals with certain medical conditions.

preparations.herb_desc: Artemisia vulgaris teas have been traditionally used as a natural remedy to treat lice infestations, particularly in children. The properties of this herbal preparation help to repel lice due to its antiparasitic and insecticidal effects, which ultimately lead to the elimination of these unwanted pests from the scalp. The bioactive constituents, including artemisinin and sesquiterpene lactones, are responsible for the antimicrobial and antiparasitic properties of Artemisia vulgaris teas, enabling them to effectively target and kill lice and their eggs. The benefits of using Artemisia vulgaris teas to treat lice infestations include their natural origin, non-toxicity, and ease of preparation, making them an attractive alternative to chemical-based treatments.
preparation_procedure: Gather 2 tablespoons of dried Artemisia vulgaris leaves.
Heat 1 cup of water in a pot.
Add the dried leaves to the water and bring to a boil.
Reduce heat and let it simmer for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the tea and let it cool before use as a lice treatment.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Artemisia vulgaris teas can be used to treat lice infestations, but possible side effects include stomach upset, dizziness, and allergic reactions in some individuals, particularly those with sensitive skin or allergies. When using these teas to treat lice, it's essential to take precautions such as performing a patch test on a small area of skin before applying the treatment, avoiding the eyes and mouth, and using gloves to minimize skin contact.

preparations.herb_desc: Thymus vulgaris teas, also known as thyme tea, have been used traditionally to treat lice infestations due to their antiparasitic and anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive constituents of thyme tea, including thymol and carvacrol, help to repel and kill lice, as well as soothe the discomfort associated with the infestation. Thyme tea's ability to dry out the lice's environment and disrupt their life cycle also contributes to its effectiveness in treating this ailment. The benefits of using thymus vulgaris teas to treat lice include a natural and non-toxic alternative to chemical-based treatments, as well as the potential to reduce the risk of resistance and recurrence.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Thymus vulgaris leaves and flowers.
Add 1 cup of boiling water to a heat-resistant cup.
Add 2 tablespoons of fresh Thymus vulgaris leaves and flowers to the boiling water.
Steep the mixture for 5-10 minutes. Strain the tea before use.
Use the Thymus vulgaris tea as a final rinse after shampooing to help repel lice.
preparations.preparation_study: According to the study, Thymus vulgaris teas for lice are not mentioned, but it is actually the 'tea tree oil' which is effective against lice, not Thymus vulgaris.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Thymus vulgaris teas can be a potential remedy for treating lice infestations, but it may cause side effects such as skin irritation, allergic reactions, and gastrointestinal issues in some individuals. To use Thymus vulgaris teas safely, take precautions to avoid over-ingesting the tea, as excessive consumption may exacerbate side effects, and also be cautious when applying the tea directly to the scalp or skin, as it can cause burning sensations and irritation.

preparations.herb_desc: Silybum marianum teas, also known as St. Mary's Thistle tea, have been traditionally used to treat lice infestations due to their antiparasitic properties. The bioactive constituents present in Silybum marianum, including silymarin and flavonoids, help to destroy the lice and their eggs, thereby providing relief from the discomfort and itching associated with lice infestations. The antiparasitic properties of Silybum marianum teas help to kill the lice and their eggs, making it easier to remove them from the hair and scalp. The benefits of using Silybum marianum teas to treat lice infestations include its natural and non-toxic nature, making it a safer alternative to chemical-based treatments.
preparation_procedure: Gather 2 tablespoons of dried Silybum marianum flowers and leaves.
Place the Silybum marianum in a cup of boiling water.
Reduce heat and let it simmer for 5 minutes.
Strain the tea into another cup and let it cool.
Drink 1 cup of the tea 2-3 times a day for 2 weeks to help get rid of lice.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Silybum marianum teas can be used to treat the lice ailment, but it may cause some side effects such as allergic reactions, skin irritation, and digestive issues due to its strong and potent properties. Precautions should be taken when using Silybum marianum teas, including avoiding prolonged use, monitoring for signs of overdose, and starting with small amounts to assess individual tolerance.

preparations.herb_desc: Hydrastis canadensis teas, also known as goldenseal tea, have been traditionally used to treat the lice ailment due to their antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive constituents of goldenseal tea, including berberine and hydrastine, help to treat lice by inhibiting the growth of the parasites and reducing the inflammation caused by the bites. By using goldenseal tea, individuals can effectively eliminate the lice and their eggs, providing relief from the discomfort and itching associated with the infestation. The benefits of using goldenseal tea to treat lice include its natural and non-toxic composition, making it a safer alternative to chemical-based treatments.
preparation_procedure: Gather Hydrastis canadensis roots and a clean glass container.
Crush 1 tablespoon of Hydrastis canadensis roots into small pieces.
Add 1 cup of boiling water to the crushed roots in the glass container.
Steep the mixture for 5-7 minutes, then strain it into another container.
Let the tea cool, then use it as a final rinse after shampooing your hair to help repel lice.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Hydrastis canadensis teas can be used to treat lice ailment, but it may cause side effects such as digestive issues, allergic reactions, and skin irritation. It is essential to take precautions when using Hydrastis canadensis teas, such as avoiding its use in children, pregnant women, and people with sensitive skin, as well as monitoring the individual's response to the treatment and adjusting the dosage accordingly.

preparations.herb_desc: Salvia officinalis teas, a herbal preparation derived from the leaves of the sage plant, have been traditionally used to treat lice infestations due to their potent insecticidal and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Salvia officinalis, including thujone and carnosic acid, help to repel and kill lice, as well as inhibit their growth and development. By incorporating Salvia officinalis teas into one's hair care routine, individuals can effectively treat and prevent lice infestations, reducing the risk of transmission and alleviating associated discomfort and itching. The benefits of using Salvia officinalis teas to treat lice include a natural and non-toxic approach to parasite control, reduced risk of chemical resistance, and a soothing effect on the scalp.
preparation_procedure: Get 1 cup of fresh Salvia officinalis leaves and 1 cup of boiling water.
Steep the leaves in the boiling water for 5-7 minutes to make a strong tea.
Strain the tea into a new cup to remove the leaves. Discard the leaves.
Add 2 tablespoons of apple cider vinegar and 2 tablespoons of honey to the tea. Stir well.
Let the tea cool down. Use it as a final rinse after shampooing to help kill lice and their eggs.
preparations.preparation_study: According to Frontiers in bioscience (Landmark edition), Salvia officinalis teas for lice, unfortunately, the provided study does not mention the effectiveness of Salvia officinalis teas against lice, as it discusses the antifungal activity of Salvia officinalis essential oil against Candida fungi.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Salvia officinalis teas can be used as a potential remedy to treat lice, but it may cause side effects such as digestive issues, allergic reactions, and interactions with other medications due to its active compounds like thujone. Precautions should be taken when using Salvia officinalis teas to treat lice, including avoiding its use in pregnant or breastfeeding women, and monitoring for signs of toxicity or adverse reactions, especially when ingesting large quantities.

preparations.herb_desc: Origanum vulgare teas, also known as oregano tea, have been traditionally used to treat lice infestation due to their potent antiparasitic and insecticidal properties. The bioactive constituents of oregano tea, such as carvacrol and thymol, possess strong ovicidal and larvicidal effects, helping to kill lice eggs and nymphs, ultimately reducing the infestation. The antiseptic and antibacterial properties of oregano tea also help to prevent secondary infections and promote healing of the scalp. Regular consumption of oregano tea has been found to be an effective and natural remedy for treating lice infestation, offering a safer alternative to chemical-based treatments.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh or dried Origanum vulgare leaves.
Pour 1 cup of boiling water over the leaves in a heat-resistant cup.
Let it steep for 5-7 minutes. Then, strain the tea into another cup.
Add 1 tablespoon of apple cider vinegar to the tea and mix well.
Apply the tea to the affected area, focusing on the scalp and hair. Leave it on for 15-20 minutes before rinsing with warm water.
preparations.preparation_study: According to Journal of intercultural ethnopharmacology, Origanum vulgare teas may not be effective against lice as the essential oil from Origanum vulgare was only shown to be effective against some bacterial isolates with varying minimum inhibitory concentrations (MIC) and minimum bactericidal concentration values, and its effectiveness is influenced by the strain origin.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Origanum vulgare teas can be used to treat lice ailments, but possible side effects may include skin irritation, allergic reactions, or gastrointestinal upset due to its strong essential oils. Precautions to take include using diluted concentrations, performing patch tests before application, and avoiding prolonged exposure to the herbal preparation to minimize the risk of adverse reactions.

preparations.herb_desc: Cassia alata teas have been traditionally used to treat the lice ailment due to their antiparasitic and insecticidal properties. The herbal preparation contains bioactive constituents such as flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic compounds, which help to repel and kill lice, thereby alleviating the symptoms of the condition. The use of Cassia alata teas has been found to be effective in treating lice infestations by killing the lice eggs and larvae, thereby preventing the spread of the infestation. The benefits of using Cassia alata teas to treat lice include their natural and non-toxic nature, making them a safer alternative to chemical-based treatments.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of dried Cassia alata leaves and flowers.
Boil 1 cup of water in a pot.
Add 1 tablespoon of dried Cassia alata to the boiling water.
Steep for 5-7 minutes, then strain the mixture.
Let the tea cool down, and use it to rinse your hair to repel lice.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Cassia alata teas can be used as a natural remedy to treat lice, but it may cause side effects such as allergic reactions, skin irritation, and gastrointestinal issues in some individuals. When using Cassia alata teas to treat lice, it is essential to take precautions to avoid overusing it, as prolonged consumption may lead to liver damage and interactions with other medications, and pregnant or breastfeeding women should use it with caution.

preparation_study: According to the study, Melaleuca alternifolia teas for lice were found to be the most effective essential oil against lice.
intro_study: According to "International journal of dermatology", teas for lice treatment may be effective due to the repellent qualities of certain botanical oils, particularly tea tree oil, which showed the most efficacy in preventing head lice.
